annotations.multiple_choice_answer: Emperor Taizong Receiving the Tibetan Envoy (also called 步辇图, Bùniǎn Tú ) is a painting on silk by Yan Liben to show the friendly encounter between the Tang dynasty and Tibet. The painting is 129 centimetres (51 in) long by 38.5 centimetres (15.2 in) wide. Bunian Tu is in The Palace Museum in Beijing.

annotations.multiple_choice_answer: Brown rot (Monilinia fructicola) is a common fungal disease affecting stone fruits like almonds and peaches. It overwinters in mummified fruit and infects blossoms in spring, causing cankers and fruit rot. Control includes removing infected parts, improving air circulation, and applying fungicides like copper or sulfur.

answers.answer: Brown rot (Monilinia fructicola) is a common fungal disease affecting stone fruits like almonds and peaches. It overwinters in mummified fruit and infects blossoms in spring, causing cankers and fruit rot. Control includes removing infected parts, improving air circulation, and applying fungicides like copper or sulfur.

annotations.multiple_choice_answer: Girl With A Pearl Earring (Dutch: Meisje met de parel) is an oil painting by Dutch Golden Age painter Johannes Vermeer, dated c. 1665. Going by various names over the centuries, it became known by its present title towards the end of the 20th century after the earring worn by the girl portrayed there. The work has been in the collection of the Mauritshuis in The Hague since 1902 and has been the subject of various literary and cinematic treatments.
